Format date/time columns (DataTables renderer: datetime)

Source: R/dt2_formats.R
dt2_format_datetime.Rd

Use DataTables' built-in DataTable.render.datetime to transform date/time strings for display (and preserve sortability).

Usage

dt2_format_datetime(
  options = list(),
  col_specs,
  from = NULL,
  to = "DD/MM/YYYY",
  locale = NULL,
  def = NULL
)

Arguments

options

List of options (returned with columnDefs updated).

col_specs

Column names or 1-based indices.

from

Input format (e.g., "YYYY-MM-DD" or ISO8601 by default).

to

Output format (e.g., "DD/MM/YYYY").

locale

Optional locale (e.g., "pt-BR").

def

Optional default output if input is invalid.

Value

Modified options.

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
opts <- list(columns = c("when", "val"))
opts <- dt2_format_datetime(opts, "when", from = "YYYY-MM-DD", to = "DD/MM/YYYY", locale = "pt-BR")
} # }

On this page

DT2 is part of an ecosystem of R packages for data analysis and computational document processing
developed at the Secretaria Executiva de Monitoramento Estratégico.

Developed by André Leite, Diogo Bezerra and Hugo Medeiros
